title: Command Packaging, Build Identity, Cross-Platform Behavior, and Distribution · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for command packaging, build identity, cross-platform behavior, and distribution and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, race, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reproducible cross-platform Go Pokedex artifact. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Keep cmd entrypoint thin, internal packages directional, fixtures separate, and runtime paths independent of source checkout.
Expose semantic version, revision, build time policy, Go version, platform, and dirty-state evidence without making builds unnecessarily nondeterministic.
Test path, newline, signal, terminal, rename, permission, and architecture assumptions on supported platforms and document differences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
